name: gist
description: Effortlessly create and update GitHub Gists from Cursor with AI-generated documentation. Automatically generates descriptive README comments for your gists using Cursor AI's understanding of your code. Perfect for sharing code snippets, examples, or quick documentation. Use '/gist-create <file>' or '/gist-update <file>' skills for one-command workflows.
longDescription:
This plugin streamlines GitHub Gist creation and management with intelligent documentation:

'/gist-create <file>' Skill:
- Takes any file from your project
- Analyzes the code using Cursor AI
- Generates a descriptive README comment explaining the purpose, usage, and context
- Creates a public gist with the file content
- Adds the README as a comment on the gist
- Returns the gist URL for sharing

'/gist-update <file>' Skill:
- Updates an existing gist with new content
- Regenerates the README comment based on changes
- Maintains gist history
- Preserves the gist URL

Ideal for:
- Sharing code examples with colleagues
- Creating quick documentation snippets
- Publishing reusable utilities
- Demonstrating bugs or features
features: One-command gist creation from any file
Gist URL returned for easy sharing
usageExamples: /gist-create myfile.js - Create gist with AI-generated docs
Say 'create a gist from this file' - Natural language
Say 'update the gist for config.json' - Update existing

requiresSetup.ghCLI: Install GitHub CLI: brew install gh
requiresSetup.gistExtension: gh CLI has built-in gist support (no extension needed)
requiresSetup.cursorrules: Skills must be added to .cursorrules file
